description:
Coordinated three-site change so caller, callee, and entry-harness agree on a typed cont sig per spec.

Touch src/ir_codegen.rs:

1. build_fn_signature (≈line 760): cont fn branch currently hardcodes `(i64 [Tagged], i64) tail` and ignores param_reprs (`let _ = param_reprs;`). Replace with:
     sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(param_reprs[0].cl_type()));   // typed result
     sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));                   // self (ptr stays i64)

2. compile_fn cont-fn entry harness (≈line 2940): currently does
     let coerced = coerce_to(.., params[0], ArgRepr::Tagged, my_param_reprs[0]);
   The cont's Cranelift param IS now in my_param_reprs[0]'s repr, so:
     drop the coerce; var_map.insert(result_param.0, params[0]);
   raw_int_vars / raw_f64_vars insertion still gates on my_param_reprs[0].

3. Term::Return native branch (≈line 3243): currently coerces val to Tagged unconditionally and emits sig `(i64 [Tagged], i64) tail`. Replace with:
     let val_typed = coerce_to(.., val, from, my_return_repr);
     let mut sig = Signature::new(CallConv::Tail);
     sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(my_return_repr.cl_type()));
     sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
     sig.returns.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
     ... return_call_indirect with val_typed
   (my_return_repr is already computed as return_reprs[this_spec_id as usize].)

The wide-cont set (uniform_cont_reachable + closure_target_fns) already forces those specs' param_reprs[0] to Tagged. Narrow conts get param_reprs[0] = RawInt / RawF64 from the typer's existing build_param_reprs path. No new analysis needed.

Halt-cont compatibility: fz_halt_cont_body's sig stays (i64 [Tagged], i64) tail. main and any fn whose cont is halt-cont has return_repr = Tagged (typer-enforced because the cont's param_reprs[0] is Tagged, so this fn's return repr unifies to Tagged). Coercion to Tagged at Term::Return falls out naturally.

Acceptance:
- add1.fz add1_s2 dump: between `v3 = iadd v0, v2` and `return_call_indirect`, NO ishl_imm/bor_imm pair. The raw 42 flows directly.
- add1.fz k_2_s3 dump: between block0 entry and `call fn0(v0)`, NO sshr_imm. v0 (the RawInt result) flows directly to print.
- cargo test --workspace green.
- All four §8 fixtures pass jit / interp / aot.

comments.text:
2026-05-14 — verified already-landed via fz-ul4.27.22.3. All three
target sites use typed sigs in tree:

  - build_fn_signature cont fn branch (src/ir_codegen.rs:946):
      sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(param_reprs[0].cl_type()));
    Comment cites fz-ul4.27.22.3. No `let _ = param_reprs;`.

  - compile_fn cont-fn entry harness (line ~3283):
      b.append_block_param(entry_cl, my_param_reprs[0].cl_type());
    No Tagged coerce.

  - Term::Return native branch (line ~3675):
      let val_typed = coerce_to(.., val, from, my_return_repr);
      sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(my_return_repr.cl_type()));
    Then return_call_indirect with val_typed.

Visible acceptance: fixtures/add1.clif add1_s2 has no ishl_imm/bor_imm
between `iadd` and `return_call_indirect`. k_2_s3 has @abi
`(RawInt) -> Tagged` with no sshr at entry.

The May 13 soundness gap that blocked the two prior attempts is
closed by fz-3zx (Kleene LFP outer fixpoint over typer specs) +
fz-ntz (tagged_return_fns transitive closure). Cont keys and
producer return reprs now agree by construction.

Closing as already-landed.

title: fz-cps-narrow.1: result-param type narrowing at cont seam
